behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing, and all missing fields are reported clearly.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at a module boundary (validator gate must prevent insurance/triage/scheduling calls), not just field-check logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network required. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior is satisfied with in-process wiring and no real model/API dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake service with missing `name` and `insurance_id` using in-process fakes for downstream modules; assert error lists both missing fields and assert downstream modules were not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, >150-year-old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is pure validation/parsing logic in one boundary. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration is disqualified because no module handoff is required to expose this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB parser/validator with valid DOB, future date, very old date, and malformed strings; assert exact validation outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code and maps `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending`.
behaviors.justification: Behavior spans request-builder + insurance client + response mapper. REAL-INFRA: real payer network not required for this failure mode; stub server/client can expose payload/mapping bugs. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System disqualified because exact failure can be reproduced with in-process stubbed responses.
behaviors.test_description: Wire insurance service to a fake payer client that records outbound payload and returns each status; assert transmitted patient ID/plan code and mapped internal status values.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked `pending` and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in error propagation across modules (insurance -> orchestrator). REAL-INFRA: network outage can be simulated via client exception/timeout in-process for this behavior. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System considered, but disqualified because behavior text is about fallback semantics (`pending` + continue), not real socket-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use intake orchestrator with payer client fake that raises timeout/unreachable error; assert eligibility becomes `pending` and scheduling/confirmation path still runs.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in model input payload.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is contract wiring between triage prompt-builder/config and model client. REAL-INFRA: no real model call needed to catch payload-construction bugs. LLM-DEP: correctness here is payload content, not model judgment. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic disqualified because behavior text does not require evaluating model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke triage service with known symptoms and rubric config against a spy model client; assert request payload contains both symptom text and rubric fields exactly once and in expected structure.

behaviors.description: Live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model clinical reasoning/output quality; deterministic stubs hide this failure. REAL-INFRA may exist, but model-decision dependence is the dominant boundary. REJECTION-GATE: Integration disqualified by behavior text `live AI` and semantic requirement to classify emergency combinations correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom fixtures (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) and assert high-urgency classification meets acceptance threshold across runs.

behaviors.description: Triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Behavior crosses parser/output mapping and persistence boundary (store explanation). REAL-INFRA: local DB/in-memory repo is enough. LLM-DEP: none if using deterministic triage response fixture. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic disqualified because behavior checks storage/schema handling of explanation presence, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed fixed triage response containing score + explanation into parser and persistence layer; assert stored record includes non-empty clinician-readable explanation column/field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and rationale rather than generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output-quality/judgment content evaluation from a live model. Stubs cannot reveal generic-vs-specific reasoning failures. REJECTION-GATE: Integration disqualified because behavior text explicitly requires `live AI` explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple symptom cases; score explanations for mention of case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale using rubric-based evaluator/human review threshold, rejecting boilerplate-only outputs.

behaviors.description: Scheduling picks first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Failure is in scheduler logic + calendar data access interaction. REAL-INFRA: local DB/repo sufficient; no real OS/network required. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System disqualified because behavior can be exposed with in-process scheduler plus test calendar state.
behaviors.test_description: Seed calendar DB with ordered availability; run scheduler for high and medium urgency; assert selected slot is earliest valid one and within required time windows.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is concurrency/transaction interaction between scheduling module and shared calendar DB. REAL-INFRA: requires real concurrent writes against actual DB transaction/locking behavior in test env (still local integration boundary). LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System considered, but disqualified because no external OS/network/subprocess dependency is required beyond local DB concurrency semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting the same candidate slot against test calendar DB with real transactions/constraints; assert only one booking succeeds and no duplicate slot rows are committed.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is message composition/content selection logic; can be validated at one function/object boundary with deterministic inputs. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Integration disqualified because required fields/instructions can be fully asserted without cross-module I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test confirmation composer with fixture appointment/patient/visit-type input; assert rendered message contains all required fields and correct prep instructions.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets module orchestration with deterministic stubbed triage, so multi-module wiring is required. REAL-INFRA: no real external API/model required. LLM-DEP: explicitly removed by stubbed triage. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic disqualified by behavior text `deterministic stubbed triage output`; Workflow disqualified because no multiple real agentic decisions are exercised.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end intake orchestrator with stubbed insurance and deterministic triage stub plus test calendar store; assert all stages execute in order and final confirmation is produced.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Behavior includes live AI triage in full pipeline; correctness depends on real model output participating in downstream handoff, so Integration is too low. REAL-INFRA (real payer API) also applies, but Agentic is higher minimum due model dependence. MULTI-STEP: only one agentic decision point (triage), so Workflow is not strictly required. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow rejected because behavior does not require multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run true end-to-end intake against real payer API and live triage model for a real submission in production-like env; assert autonomous completion with persisted appointment and sent confirmation, no human intervention.
